name: Ermita Santos nuevos y Alto de Tabanera – circular desde San Andrés de Soria
region_intro: Hiking around Almarza offers diverse trails through the Soria region of Spain. The landscape is characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and river valleys, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Hikers can explore areas featuring historical sites and natural forests, with routes often following established paths. The region's topography includes gentle ascents and descents, making it accessible for different hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Almarza The most popular hiking route is Senda del Acebal Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 36 minutes to complete. This easy route leads through a notable holly forest. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castro Las Espinillas y La virgen de las Espinillas – desde Valdeavellano de Tera, a moderate 2.7 miles (4.4 km) path. This trail features historical fortresses and scenic views. Local hikers also love the Castro Alto de la Cruz Loop, a 5.4 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with historical points of interest, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Almarza is defined by forested paths, riverine landscapes, and historical sites, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural exploration. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Almarza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 60 hikers have used komoot to explore Almarza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Almarza?
A: Almarza offers a selection of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, including dense woodlands, river valleys, and areas with historical significance.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 8 easy hiking trails around Almarza. A great option for families or beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063912767/senda-del-acebal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Acebal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.4 miles (5.5 km) long and leads through a notable holly forest.

Q: What about more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Almarza features 2 difficult trails. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062391615/ermita-santos-nuevos-y-alto-de-tabanera-circular-desde-san-andres-de-soria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita Santos nuevos y Alto de Tabanera – circular desde San Andrés de Soria&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 14-mile (22.5 km) circular hike with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Almarza?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063933801/castro-alto-de-la-cruz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Alto de la Cruz Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.4-mile (8.6 km) trail that takes you through varied terrain and past historical points of interest, returning to your starting point.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Almarza?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Almarza region. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural environment.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The trails around Almarza offer a mix of natural beauty and historical sites. You can discover highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3936758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge over the Tera River&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3992864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Gallina Spring&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Oro&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass through dense woodlands and along riverine landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore on the trails?
A: Yes, the Almarza region is rich in history. You can explore ancient fortresses and archaeological sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4000052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Honda Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063880639/castro-las-espinillas-y-la-virgen-de-las-espinillas-desde-valdeavellano-de-tera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Las Espinillas y La virgen de las Espinillas – desde Valdeavellano de Tera&lt;/a&gt; trail, for instance, features historical fortresses and scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Almarza?
A: The best time for hiking in Almarza is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant with wildflowers or autumn foliage. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, but be prepared for warmer temperatures.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many routes starting from villages like Almarza or Valdeavellano de Tera offer designated parking areas or roadside parking. It's recommended to check specific route details on komoot for information on parking at your chosen starting point.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Almarza?
A: The hiking trails around Almarza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 6 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that make for enjoyable outdoor experiences.

Q: Are there any places to eat or get refreshments near the trails?
A: While many trails venture into natural areas, you'll find cafes and restaurants in the villages surrounding Almarza, such as Almarza itself or Valdeavellano de Tera. It's a good idea to plan your route to pass through or end near these villages if you're looking for refreshments.

Q: Are there any mountain shelters or huts along the trails?
A: Yes, there are several huts and shelters in the region that can provide a resting spot or refuge. Notable examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3956862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Valonsadero Cabin&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5587268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Turruca Stone Hut&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6822545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Road Workers' Shelter&lt;/a&gt;. These can be useful for longer hikes.
tips: Located at 1,252 meters above sea level, the Hermitage of Santos Nuevos offers us a beautiful picture. Nearby is the beautiful construction, we find a picnic area under the shade, ideal for taking a break and recovering energy.
Beautiful picture that we are at the top of the Alto de Tabanera surrounded by tall pines that mark the way.
Walking through the 1,598 masl we find a set of three lagoons that offer us a great attraction to add to our route.
